calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2016 Off Calendar

Notice Of Defendants Special Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT ZAID FAROOQUI'S (Special Anti-SLAPP) Motion To Strike Complaint (CCP 425.16) is DENIED in its entirety. While Mr. Mr. Farooqui has satisfied his prong one burden of showing that Mr. Kaplan's claims arise from protected activity because they are based on consumer warnings that are connected to a public concern (Wilbanks v. Wolk, (2004) 121 Cal.App.4th 883), Mr. Kaplan has satisfied his prong two burden of showing the minimal merit of all of this claims. The evidence submitted by Mr. Kaplan shows that Mr. Farooqui knowingly made false and defamatory statements about Mr. Kaplan that harmed Mr. Kaplan and that none of the claims asserted by Mr. Kaplan are time-barred. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

Off Calendar Mar-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID B. GOLDEN, ESQ. of Golden - Kopcke, Tel No. 415-399-9994 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. ANDREW S. CANTOR, ESQ. of Law Office of Andrew S. Cantor, Tel No. 415-817-1708 appearing for Defendant Zaif Farooqui.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT ZAIF FAROOQUI'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment, Defendant's motion to set aside the default judgment will, in the interests of justice and judicial economy, be treated as also seeking to set aside the default and, as so treated, is granted. Defendant established sufficient grounds to set aside both the default and default judgment on the grounds of both lack of service of summons and plaintiff's lack of knowledge of the lawsuit. Moreover, the strong public policy favoring resolution of lawsuits on their merits favors granting the motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189 (302/HK)
